title: ranunculus scotinus
intro: Ranunculus scotinus, also known as Scottish buttercup, is a perennial plant species in the family Ranunculaceae. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Ranunculus scotinus are said to include wound healing, relief from rheumatism and arthritis, and the treatment of digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tincture, infusion, or salve, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
